warnings: Source documents are included only when explicitly selected.
Secret values are not exported; only secret references are included.
Template data is starter evidence, not locked contract truth.
Large developer evidence markdown is intentionally omitted; reusable developer evidence is carried by structured backend operations and capability mappings.

template.title: Jira Fronting Starter
template.summary: Starter template for a governed Jira fronting ANIP service.
template.description: Imports Jira fronting intent, integration evidence, connection refs, backend operations, and capability mappings as starter evidence for a governed Jira ANIP service. The heavy developer evidence remains represented as structured mappings and backend operations rather than a large markdown blob.
template.recommendedBrief: Canonical Jira fronting showcase package for governed Jira reads, preview-only preparation, approval-gated changes, audit evidence, and safe denial of raw tool access.

documents.title: Jira integration evidence
documents.content:
# Jira Integration Evidence Template

> Imported from starter template `jira-fronting-starter`. Verify every operation against your chosen native API/MCP source before locking.

Native API connection: JIRA_BASE_URL
MCP connection: ATLASSIAN_MCP_ENDPOINT

## Template-suggested backend operations
- jira.rest.search_issues: GET /jira/backlog/context/search
- jira.rest.get_issue: GET /jira/issues/{issue_key}/context
- jira.rest.get_comments: GET /jira/issues/{issue_key}/context
- jira.rest.get_changelog: GET /jira/issues/{issue_key}/context
- jira.rest.get_create_metadata: POST /jira/incidents/bugs/preview
- jira.rest.create_issue: POST /jira/incidents/bugs/preview
- jira.rest.get_create_metadata: POST /jira/stories/preview
- jira.rest.create_issue: POST /jira/stories/preview
- jira.rest.get_issue: POST /jira/issues/{parent_issue_key}/subtasks/preview
- jira.rest.create_issue: POST /jira/issues/{parent_issue_key}/subtasks/preview
- jira.rest.get_issue: POST /jira/issues/{issue_key}/customer-escalation-comment/preview
- jira.rest.add_comment: POST /jira/issues/{issue_key}/customer-escalation-comment/preview
- jira.rest.get_transitions: POST /jira/issues/{issue_key}/workflow-transition/request
- jira.rest.transition_issue: POST /jira/issues/{issue_key}/workflow-transition/request
- jira.rest.get_boards: POST /jira/sprints/move-request
- jira.rest.get_sprints: POST /jira/sprints/move-request
- jira.rest.move_issues_to_sprint: POST /jira/sprints/move-request
- jira.rest.assign_issue: POST /jira/issues/{issue_key}/assignee-change/request
- jira.rest.user_search: POST /jira/issues/{issue_key}/assignee-change/request
- jira.rest.get_issue_link_types: POST /jira/issues/link-request
- jira.rest.link_issues: POST /jira/issues/link-request
- jira.rest.search_issues: GET /jira/releases/{release_ref}/notes/preview
- jira.rest.get_versions: GET /jira/releases/{release_ref}/notes/preview

## Secret posture
- Studio stores secret references only; project exports must not contain tokens.
- Teams can implement either native API or MCP backend seams behind the same governed ANIP contract.

documents.title: Jira governed fronting intent
documents.content:
# Jira Governed Fronting Intent

> Imported from starter template `jira-fronting-starter`. Treat this as draft guidance, not discovered truth.

Expose a governed Jira capability surface in front of native API or MCP-backed operations.

## Template-suggested capabilities
- jira.backlog.search_context: Find bounded work context in an allowed Jira project without exposing raw JQL or unbounded issue export.
- jira.issue.get_context: Inspect one Jira issue with bounded actor-visible fields, comments, and history.
- jira.incident_bug.prepare: Prepare a Jira bug ticket from incident or defect context without creating it.
- jira.story.prepare: Prepare a Jira story preview with acceptance criteria before any issue is created.
- jira.subtask.prepare: Prepare a subtask under an existing parent issue without creating it.
- jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare: Prepare a customer-safe Jira comment from incident or triage context without posting it.
- jira.workflow_transition.request: Request a governed Jira issue transition and stop for approval when policy requires it.
- jira.sprint_move.request: Request moving Jira issue(s) into a sprint or backlog with approval and actor-visible board scope.
- jira.assignee_change.request: Request reassignment of a Jira issue with backend user resolution, preview, approval, and audit.
- jira.issue_link.request: Request linking two Jira issues with a declared relationship and cross-project policy checks.
- jira.release_notes.prepare: Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issues without publishing or mutating Jira.

## Governance posture
- Raw Jira tools are not exposed directly to agents.
- Reads return bounded actor-visible context.
- Write-adjacent actions prepare previews or require explicit approval before execution.
- Missing required inputs trigger clarification instead of guessing.

## User-provided project brief
Create the canonical Jira governed fronting showcase. The ANIP service must expose business capabilities for bounded Jira context, issue preparation, workflow and sprint-change previews, assignee-change requests, issue-link requests, release-note preparation, approval-required write-adjacent actions, explicit denials for raw Jira/JQL/tool access, and audit evidence. The agent must see governed capabilities, not raw Jira REST or MCP operations.
